speaker: ANNE
text: Not for that neither. Here’s the pang that pinches:

speaker: ANNE
text: His Highness having lived so long with her, and she

speaker: ANNE
text: So good a lady that no tongue could ever

speaker: ANNE
text: Pronounce dishonor of her—by my life,

speaker: ANNE
text: She never knew harm-doing!—O, now, after

speaker: ANNE
text: So many courses of the sun enthroned,

speaker: ANNE
text: Still growing in a majesty and pomp, the which

speaker: ANNE
text: To leave a thousandfold more bitter than

speaker: ANNE
text: ’Tis sweet at first t’ acquire—after this process,

speaker: ANNE
text: To give her the avaunt! It is a pity

speaker: ANNE
text: Would move a monster.

OLD
               
              LADY
text: Hearts of most hard temper

OLD
               
              LADY
text: Melt and lament for her.

speaker: ANNE
text: O, God’s will! Much better

speaker: ANNE
text: She ne’er had known pomp; though ’t be temporal,

speaker: ANNE
text: Yet if that quarrel, Fortune, do divorce

speaker: ANNE
text: It from the bearer, ’tis a sufferance panging

speaker: ANNE
text: As soul and body’s severing.

              LADY
text: Alas, poor lady,

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: She’s a stranger now again!

speaker: ANNE
text: So much the more

speaker: ANNE
text: Must pity drop upon her. Verily,

speaker: ANNE
text: I swear, ’tis better to be lowly born

speaker: ANNE
text: And range with humble livers in content

speaker: ANNE
text: Than to be perked up in a glist’ring grief

speaker: ANNE
text: And wear a golden sorrow.

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: Our content

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: Is our best having.

speaker: ANNE
text: By my troth and maidenhead,

speaker: ANNE
text: I would not be a queen.

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: Beshrew me, I would,

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: And venture maidenhead for ’t; and so would you,

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: For all this spice of your hypocrisy.

OLD
               
              LADY
text: You, that have so fair parts of woman on you,

speaker:
OLD

text: Have too a woman’s heart, which ever yet

speaker:
OLD

text: Saving your mincing, the capacity

text: Of your soft cheveril conscience would receive

text: If you might please to stretch it.

speaker: ANNE
text: Nay, good troth.

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: Yes, troth, and troth. You would not be a queen?

speaker: ANNE
text: No, not for all the riches under heaven.

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: ’Tis strange. A threepence bowed would hire me,

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: Old as I am, to queen it. But I pray you,

              LADY
text: What think you of a duchess? Have you limbs

              LADY

speaker: ANNE
text: No, in truth.

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: Then you are weakly made. Pluck off a little.

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: I would not be a young count in your way

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: For more than blushing comes to. If your back

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: Cannot vouchsafe this burden, ’tis too weak

speaker:
OLD
               
              LADY
text: Ever to get a boy.

speaker: ANNE
text: How you do talk!

speaker: ANNE
text: I swear again, I would not be a queen

speaker: ANNE
text: For all the world.

OLD

text: In faith, for little England

text: Good morrow, ladies. What were ’t worth to know

text: The secret of your conference?

speaker: ANNE
text: My good lord,

speaker: ANNE
text: Not your demand; it values not your asking.

speaker: ANNE
text: Our mistress’ sorrows we were pitying.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: It was a gentle business, and becoming

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: The action of good women. There is hope

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: All will be well.

speaker: ANNE
text: Now, I pray God, amen!

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: You bear a gentle mind, and heav’nly blessings

text: Follow such creatures. That you may, fair lady,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Perceive I speak sincerely, and high note’s

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Ta’en of your many virtues, the King’s Majesty

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Commends his good opinion of you to you, and

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Does purpose honor to you no less flowing

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Than Marchioness of Pembroke, to which title

text: A thousand pound a year annual support

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Out of his grace he adds.

speaker: ANNE
text: I do not know

speaker: ANNE
text: What kind of my obedience I should tender.

speaker: ANNE
text: More than my all is nothing, nor my prayers

speaker: ANNE
text: Are not words duly hallowed, nor my wishes

speaker: ANNE
text: More worth than empty vanities. Yet prayers and wishes

speaker: ANNE
text: Are all I can return. ’Beseech your Lordship,

speaker: ANNE
text: Vouchsafe to speak my thanks and my obedience,

speaker: ANNE
text: As from a blushing handmaid, to his Highness,

speaker: ANNE
text: Whose health and royalty I pray for.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Lady,

text: I shall not fail t’ approve the fair conceit

text:
The King hath of you.

               I have perused her well.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Beauty and honor in her are so mingled

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: That they have caught the King. And who knows yet

text: Make yourself mirth with your particular fancy,
